goals.description:
Future-Ready, Deeper Learning: Support Each Child in Demonstrating the Skills and Characteristics
described in our CUSD Portrait of a Learner (POL), including foundational academic content skills
as well as our POL Competencies: Inclusive Collaborator; Global Contributor; Effective
Communicator; Critical Thinker; Resilient Learner; Inspired Creator

metrics.metric_name:
Percentage of Fully
Credentialed and
Appropriately Assigned
Teachers (Assignment
monitoring outcome of
"Clear", per District HR
Records)
metrics.baseline_raw:
2023-2024
As of February 14,
2024, 100% of CUSD
teachers held a
credential that
authorized them to
teach in their
assignment per CTC.
As of February 14, 2024
fourteen (14) of the total
675 teachers were not
fully credentialed, giving
CUSD a rate of 98%
fully credentialed
teachers. Teachers who
are not fully
credentialed are
working towards
achieving their
credential within 12
months of hiring.
metrics.year_1_outcome_raw:
2024-2025
As of January 29,
2025, 99.8% of
CUSD teachers
hold a credential
that authorizes
them to teach in
their assignment
per CTC. As of
January 29, 2025,
eight (8) of the
total 699 teachers
are not fully
credentialed,
giving CUSD a
rate of 98.8% fully
credentialed
teachers.
Teachers who are
not fully
credentialed are
working towards
achieving their
credentials within
12 months of
hiring.
metrics.year_3_target_raw:
2026-2027
Maintain 98% or
higher
metrics.current_difference_from_baseline_raw:
Fully credentialed
teachers: +1%

actions.title:
Reading Intervention
Teachers
actions.description:
Provide targeted instruction and tiered supports in order to help all children
meet or exceed grade level standards in reading. Intervention teachers will
prioritize support for English Learners, Low Income and Foster Youth
students who are performing below grade level to improve reading skills
and support success in all content areas. This action includes our
elementary and middle school reading intervention teachers. Please note:
This action also is part of our Coordinated Early Intervening Services
(CCEIS) services action plan which is designed to reduce over
identification of Hispanic students in special education. Beginning in 2025-
2026, this action will include supporting the implementation of a new
reading screener, per state requirements. Reading Intervention teachers
will assist with professional development to support this implementation.

actions.title:
Math Intervention
Teachers
actions.description:
Provide targeted instruction and tiered supports in order to help all children
meet or exceed grade level standards in mathematics Intervention
teachers will prioritize support for English Learners, Low Income and
Foster Youth students who are performing below grade level to improve
skills and support success. This action includes intervention teachers at
our elementary and middle schools. Please note: This action also is part of
our Coordinated Early Intervening Services (CCEIS) services action plan
which is designed to reduce over identification of Hispanic students in
special education.

actions.title:
English Learner
Support
actions.description:
Provide resources and support to help EL students reach proficiency in
English and reclassify as Redesignated English Proficient (R-FEP), which
means they are no longer English Learners. This includes our Ellevation
Digital Platform that provides instructional strategies as well as data and
process management for reclassification and monitoring R-FEP students.
This also includes our staff analyst who coordinates reclassification.

actions.title:
Leadership Support
for Principals and
actions.description:
Directors: Support principals and teachers in building tiered interventions
and using data to monitor student progress for unduplicated pupils.
Professional
Development for
Teachers
Directors provide professional development and leadership coaching to
improve the academic performance of unduplicated pupils. Directors assist
principals in developing SPSAs and taking action to shift instructional
practices at their sites. This action also includes our Data Standards and
Assessment Analyst position who supports Directors, principals and school
sites in accessing the data they need to make student-centered decisions,
including placing students in interventions.

actions.title:
Instructional
Coaching for
Teachers
actions.description:
Instructional Coaches: Provide coaching in MTSS and Portrait of a Learner
to support future-ready learning for students, a personalized approach to
instruction, and a positive, engaging classroom environment. Coaches
prioritize strategies for meeting the needs of unduplicated pupils. In 2025-
2206, all coaches will have a role in supporting the implementation of
future-ready, deeper learning across all sites. This will include supporting
professional development and teacher collaboration.

actions.title:
iReady/NWEA
Diagnostic and
Personalized
Learning Pathway
actions.description:
Provide iReady in Grades 1-5 and NWEA in Grades 6-8 to support
personalized learning in order to maximize opportunities to students to gain
skills that they need in order to master grade level content in reading and
mathematics. This tool provides a diagnostic assessment as well as
personalized learning pathways to support all students in meeting
academic achievement goals. Our unduplicated students perform below
grade level at a higher rate than our All Student group and this tool allows
us to identify needs, prioritize targeted instruction and provide a pathway
for success. Results are shared with parents as our partners in their child's
education. This work facilitates goal setting and progress monitoring for
unduplicated students. Staff members use this data in the SPSA and in the
LCAP, as well as formatively to inform teaching practices, identify focal
areas and develop improvement plans.

actions.title:
Elevate Math
Summer Support
Program
actions.description:
Provide extended learning time (summer) for students who need more
instruction to master grade level skills in Math. Provide supplies and
contract costs to offer this program.

actions.title:
Instructional
Assistants (IAs) for
Reading Intervention
actions.description:
Provide IAs to support reading intervention as part of our Tier 2 system of
support

actions.title:
EL Resources for
Sites 2 or more
Levels Below District
on the CA Dashboard
actions.description:
Provide instructional assistant support for newcomer ELs at sites with more
than 10% ELs according to the 2024 State Dashboard. Listenwise
application for all middle school EL students $14,520 out of Title III. Read
1 8 0 materials for all middle school English Learners and Language Launch
for middle school EL newcomers, Benchmark Hello curriculum for all
elementary newcomer English Language students. Create Student
Success Plans and review exit criteria (or hold IEP meetings for SWD who
are also LTELs) for all Long Term English Learners (LTELs) at school sites
to develop personalized learning plans to support progress towards
reclassification. Provide professional development to principals so that they
can support their site teams in developing these plans. (Note: Please see
our Federal Addendum for ELD professional development plans)

actions.title:
Future-Ready
Learning Networks
actions.description:
Foster professional learning and collaboration to support the advancement
o f our Portrait of a Learner and Future-Ready Learning (Funds provided by
Educator Effectiveness Funds). In 2025-2026, this will include the addition
of minimum days to form teacher networks within CUSD which will allow
teachers the opportunity to learn, collaborate and plan together to
implement future-ready, deeper learning in their classrooms. This also will
include exploratory work to determine if school gardens can be a vehicle
for expanding our work in Future-Ready, Deeper Learning. Additionally, we
will add a Portrait of a Learner Community Advisory "Think Tank" to guide
our next steps in this essential area.

actions.title:
Updated Textbooks:
World Language &
Science
actions.description:
COMPLETED: Provide updated middle school World Language Textbooks
for Spanish and French electives, aligned to new state framework. Provide
updated elementary science textbooks for Grades 3-5 (This action was
completed in 2024-2025; therefore, no expenses are indicated for 2025-
2026)
actions.contributing_raw:
No
XStudents Enrolled in French
or Spanish World Language
electives; All Students in
Grades 3-5 for S cience
Materials

actions.title:
Sonday Coaching for
Special Education
Teachers
actions.description:
COMPLETED: Provide coaching in Sonday (reading curriculum) for
Mild/Moderate, SAI and Resource special education teachers (This action
was completed in 2024-2025; therefore, no expenses are indicated for
2025-2026)

actions.title:
GoalBook for Special
Education
actions.description:
Provide case managers and other special education staff access to
GoalBoook to support evidence-based instructional practices and
strategies for developing effective IEPs for students. Provide training and
support to implement this new tool.

actions.title: AI Tools
actions.description:
Explore AI tools to support personalized student learning and teacher
lesson planning to address a range of student needs

actions.title: Technology Devices
actions.description:
E n sure that all students have access to a technology device less than 5
years old. Replenish aging devices to maintain student access to updated
technology to support learning.

actions.title: School Library Books
actions.description:
M a intain and expand school library collections to meet the needs of
diverse readers. This will include books as well as supplies exclusively for
school libraries. (Please note: American Library Association recommends
15-20 books per student. The California Department of Education indicates
that the average copyright is 20 years old.)

actions.title:
Digital Tools to
Support Personalized
Learning
actions.description:
Digital Tools to support teachers in meeting the unique needs of each
learner and maximize opportunities to students to gain skills that they need
in order to master grade level content in reading, writing and mathematics.
These tools will support all students in meeting academic achievement
goals. Our unduplicated students perform below grade level at a higher
rate than our All Student group and these tools allows us to identify needs,
prioritize targeted instruction and provide pathways for success. Tools
include: Writeable, ESGI, Literably, RAZ-Kids, Newsela, Brain Pop,
Seesaw, Screencastify, Pear Deck, Kami, Overdrive, Typing Club and
Magic School.

actions.title:
Leadership Support for
Principals and Professional
Development for Teachers

goals.description:
Whole Child: Help students thrive socially, emotionally and behaviorally by fostering engaging,
positive, safe learning environments where everyone belongs.

actions.title:
Student Success
Liaisons
actions.description:
Provide tiered supports in order to help all children demonstrate expected
behaviors and feel a sense of belonging at school. Continue Student
Success Liaisons at our middle school campuses with highest needs
(reduced from 12 month to 10 month positions for 2025-2026). A
systematic approach will support our EL, FY and Low Income students to
thrive. As noted in our CHKS data, students in these groups often need
additional support in the area of SEL and we believe that system-wide
focused, positive, teaching will support an increased sense of self-efficacy
and improved success at school for our students. The importance of these
supports also was highlighted in our partner feedback from parents and
staff.

actions.title:
Counseling/Mental
Health
actions.description:
Provide Mental Health Support for students at all 23 sites. Provide Mental
Health Support for students at all 23 sites. This includes Mental Health
Therapists and Interns. According to our CHKS data, absenteeism rates
and our student achievement data, EL, Low Income and Foster Youth
students would benefit from supports to increase engagement in order to
achieve long term success. Counseling provides an essential role in MTSS
to support positive behavior and life skills as well as academic gains.
Services will be improved because we are transitioning from contracted
employees to CUSD employees who will build relationships and provide
consistency over time.

actions.title:
Support Homeless
(Low Income) and
Foster Youth
actions.description:
Provide supplies and resources to low income, homeless and foster youth
students in order to support their success at school. Provide bus tokens
and for homeless students who need transportation in order to attend
school regularly. Supporting our Homeless (Low Income) and Foster Youth
families with basic resources will help students attend school regularly and
engage in their learning.

actions.title: PBIS
actions.description: Train all sites in PBIS to support safe, positive school climates

actions.title: Nursing Support
actions.description:
Provide a Nurse Coordinator to ensure that our health team is supported to
meet each child's wellness needs.

actions.title:
Elementary Music
and Middle School
Arts Electives
actions.description:
Expand weekly music instruction to include all TK-5 students (This part of
the action was completed in 2024-2025). Build upon long-standing music
program in Grades 4-5 to add grades TK-3. Provide funds for arts supplies
and electives in middle school (Note: Funding Source is Prop 28; Metric
2.7)

actions.title:
Attendance
Messaging, Protocols
and Site Teams
actions.description:
Address Chronic Absenteeism in order to ensure positive student
engagement in learning. Implement attendance campaign messaging as
well as attendance review protocols to monitor student engagement. While
no student groups or schools are "Red" on the 2024 CA Dashboard, the
following schools had student groups in the "Red" on the 2023 CA
Dashboard, and additional resources will be provided to principals
(coaching, communication tools) to continue to address these areas in their
SPSAs: Blue Hills, Eaton, Nimitz, Sedgwick, Eisenhower, Collins,
Stocklmeir, DeVargas, Stevens Creek, Cupertino Middle, Kennedy Middle.
These sites also will form student attendance teams to monitor progress
and address needs.
Additional details regarding student groups within a school within the LEA
that received the lowest performance level on one or more state indicators
on the 2023 Dashboard:
Many of our schools had student groups in "Red" for Chronic Absenteeism,
as follows: Blue Hills Elementary: Socio-economically Disadvantaged
Students; Collins Elementary: All Students; DeVargas Elementary: English
Learners, Socio-economically Disadvantaged Students, Students with
Disabilities, Hispanic, White; Eaton Elementary: English Learners; Nimitz
Elementary: 2 or more Races; Cupertino Middle School: English Learners,
Students with Disabilities, Hispanic; Sedgwick Elementary: Students with
Disabilities and Hispanic; Eisenhower Elementary: Socio-economically
Disadvantaged Students, Students with Disabilities, 2 or More Races;
Kennedy Middle School: Students with Disabilities; Stevens Creek
Elementary: All Students Socio-economically Disadvantaged Students,
Students with Disabilities, Stocklmeir Elementary: Students with Disabilities
Note: See Action 2.3 for information about how we will continue to address
the student group within CUSD that received the lowest performance level
on one or more state indicators on the 2023 Dashboard: Homeless
students were identified as "Red" for Chronic Absenteeism. Our Director of
Student Support Services is working with school sites and families to
address this issue, identify root causes, and increase supports.

actions.title:
SST Meetings & Tier
1/Tier 2 Site Teams
actions.description:
Updated protocols for Student Study Team (SST) meetings will be
im plemented consistently across the District as part of our commitment to
MTSS. Ensure that each site has a Tier 1/Tier 2 team (For example CST,
IST) Train site staff in how to use these protocols to identify student needs,
apply interventions, and track progress. All LTEL students will have an
SST to develop a success plan along with progress monitoring. This is a
no cost action as it will be provided by in-house staff during regularly
scheduled work time.

actions.title:
Social-Emotional
Learning (SEL) TK-5
and 6-8; CHKS
survey tools to
measure needs and
progress
actions.description:
Utilize Second Step instructional materials for grades TK-5; Identify a
consistent set of learning goals and supplemental resources to teach
social-emotional learning skills in Grades 6-8; Utilize California Health Kids
Survey (CHKS) to identify needs and measure progress

actions.title: ELOP
actions.description:
Provide the Expanded Learning Opportunities Program at all school sites
to serve eligible students in grades TK-6 (EL, Foster Youth, Low Income).
This item includes vendor contracts, CUSD staff and snacks for students.

actions.title:
After School Sports
(Middle School)
actions.description:
Continue Middle School After School Sports to support wellness and a
sense of belonging for students. This action funds two District Athletic
Directors to coordinate this program as well as an Ed. Services Director to
manage the program.

actions.title:
Therapeutic Crisis
Intervention Training
actions.description:
Train all BIs and Principals. Offer monthly training for any staff member
w h o wants to be trained. Completed in 24-25: This action funds CUSD staff
to become Trainers who can provide in-house professional development.
For 25-26, our in-house trainers will continue to provide training for CUSD
staff. This is now a no cost item.

actions.title:
Behavior Support
Team (General
Education)
actions.description:
Develop and implement a new Behavior Support Team to provide short-
term General Education support to sites managing extreme student
behaviors. This team will include a Mental Health Therapist, a Behavior
Supervisor and an Instructional Coach. All of these staff members are
funded under different areas of the LCAP and District budget; therefore this
is a no cost action focused on development of internal systems and
processes

goals.description:
Community Engagement: Actively engage parents and community members in supporting the
implementation of the CUSD Strategic Plan, including the Portrait of a Learner

actions.title: Parent Education
actions.description:
Provide parent education to support families to learn about the District and
partner to help their child make progress. This will include a menu of virtual
events addressing topics such as developmental stages, social-emotional
learning, well-being, and digital safety. Additionally, we will prioritize
engaging with parents in the implementation of Future-Ready Learning and
our Portrait of a Learner.

actions.title: Translation
actions.description:
Provide translation services to support families in engaging with school and
District personnel. Parents who are English Learners need these supports
in order to participate in school meetings. This is critical for many of our
parents of EL students to meaningfully engage as our educational partners
in support of their child's success. This includes translation support in our
Student Assignment Office.

actions.title:
Parent Liaisons and
Support
actions.description:
Provide Parent Liaisons, support from our Senior Director of
Communication, as well as an Engagement and Community Resource
Specialist to support families of unduplicated students in need. These
positions focus on linking families with school and community resources,
facilitating conversations between families and school personnel, and
supporting improved student attendance. Our EL, Low Income and Foster
Youth families primarily benefit from these resources. Supporting our
unduplicated families in accessing community resources is a high priority in
CUSD because we know that these resources can provide pathways to
success and well-being. (Note: In 2025-2026, Parent Liaison hours will be
concentrated at our sites receiving Title I funds. These sites are DeVargas
Elementary and Nimitz Elementary. Other supports listed in this action will
be shared across all CUSD sites).

actions.title:
Project Cornerstone
Action Title
actions.description:
S u pport social-emotional learning, literacy and positive home-school
partnerships. Project Cornerstone is a supplemental program offered to
sites. Programs that support SEL skill development and promote positive
decision-making can support long-term student success as well as
increased sense of belonging at school.
